schema.description: Representation of a Time Off Policy

uuid.description: Unique identifier of a time off policy

company_uuid.description: Unique identifier for the company owning the time off policy

name.description: Name of the time off policy

policy_type.description: Type of the time off policy

accrual_rate.description: The rate at which the time off hours will accrue for an employee on the policy. Represented as a float, e.g. "40.0".

accrual_rate_unit.description: The number of hours an employee has to work or be paid for to accrue the number of hours set in the accrual rate. Only used for hourly policies (per_hour_paid, per_hour_paid_no_overtime, per_hour_work, per_hour_worked_no_overtime). Represented as a float, e.g. "40.0".

paid_out_on_termination.description: Boolean representing if an employee's accrued time off hours will be paid out on termination

accrual_waiting_period_days.description: Number of days before an employee on the policy will begin accruing time off hours

carryover_limit_hours.description: The max number of hours an employee can carryover from one year to the next

max_accrual_hours_per_year.description: The max number of hours an employee can accrue in a year

max_hours.description: The max number of hours an employee can accrue

policy_reset_date.description: The date the policy resets. Format MM-DD

complete.description: boolean representing if a policy has completed configuration

version.description: The current version of the object. See the [versioning guide](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/versioning#object-layer) for information on how to use this field. The version will be null if the policy is no longer active.

is_active.description: boolean representing if a policy is active or not

employees.description: List of employee UUIDs under a time off policy

balance.description: The time off balance for the employee

schema.title: Time-Off-Policy

schema.description:
Not Found 
  
The requested resource does not exist. Make sure the provided ID/UUID is valid.

error_key.description: Specifies where the error occurs. Typically this key identifies the attribute/parameter related to the error.

category.description: Specifies the type of error. The category provides error groupings and can be used to build custom error handling in your integration.

message.description: Provides details about the error - generally this message can be surfaced to an end user.

errors.message: The requested resource was not found.

schema.title: Not-Found-Error-Object
